article_meta.title: close Video American Accountability Foundation president releases Education Department 'watch list,' explains why Trump

reasoning: The model returned an empty array for an article that contains several instances of author-level bias. While the article is heavily quote-driven and sourced from government statements, the author's own prose contains notable bias patterns that should have been flagged.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model returned an empty array, so there are zero attribution rule violations. No quoted text was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No items were flagged at all, so there are no false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed several defensible bias instances in the author's own prose: (1) 'so-called book bans' - the author uses this loaded framing in their own voice (Word Choice); (2) 'Book Ban Hoax' - while attributed to the department, the author frames it without scare quotes or distancing language in places, adopting the department's framing; (3) The phrase 'working to protect students from obscene content' appears to be the author's paraphrase adopting the department's characterization (Word Choice/Spin); (4) Slant/Bias by Omission - the article presents only the DOE/administration perspective with no counterpoint from free speech advocates, librarians, or those who filed the original complaints; (5) 'age-inappropriate, sexually explicit or obscene materials' in the author's own connecting prose adopts the characterization of one side. The most significant miss is the Slant/Bias by Omission - the article is entirely one-sided with no opposing perspective.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No labels were applied, so accuracy cannot be assessed negatively, scored at 1 by default.
